mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment signals.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) signal a tightening cycle, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show positive correlation with combined FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increases in 60%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals weakening economic demand, triggering recessionary fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in GDPC1 decline periods exceed 10% annualized in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strengthening supply-side capacity, reducing inflationary pressures and enabling positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in INDPRO rise periods show negative correlation with industrial output growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects stronger consumer demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in HOUST rise periods fall below 0.5% average in 60%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show negative correlation with combined RSAFS and HOUST growth in 50%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates labor market weakness, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in PAYEMS decline periods exceed 1.5% average in 60%+ of cases.
